title: CRP biosensor — auto save E2E
hypothesis: A paper-based electrochemical biosensor functionalized with anti-CRP antibodies will detect C-reactive protein in whole blood at concentrations below 0.5 mg/L within 10 minutes, matching laboratory ELISA sensitivity without requiring sample preprocessing.

parsed_hypothesis.intervention: paper-based electrochemical biosensor functionalized with anti-CRP antibodies
paper-based electrochemical biosensor functionalized with anti-CRP antibodies

novelty.rationale: There are references discussing electrochemical biosensors and CRP detection, such as reference 5 which mentions real-time detection of CRP using electrochemical impedance spectroscopy. However, none of the references specifically describe a paper-based electrochemical biosensor functionalized with anti-CRP antibodies for detecting CRP in whole blood at the specified sensitivity and time frame.

executive_summary.objective: To evaluate the analytical performance of a paper-based electrochemical biosensor functionalized with anti-CRP antibodies for detecting C-reactive protein in whole blood.
executive_summary.experimental_strategy: Fabricate the biosensor, functionalize with anti-CRP antibodies, and test its sensitivity and detection time against a standard laboratory ELISA.
executive_summary.expected_result: The biosensor will detect CRP at concentrations below 0.5 mg/L within 10 minutes, matching ELISA sensitivity.
executive_summary.decision_gate: Proceed to full-scale testing if biosensor meets sensitivity and time criteria.

safety_ethics_compliance.human_subjects_or_samples: Use of human blood samples requires ethical approval.
waste_disposal: Biohazard waste disposal for human samples
critical_warnings: Ensure proper handling of human blood samples to prevent contamination.

protocol.title: Fabrication of Paper-Based Biosensor
protocol.purpose: To create the paper-based scaffold for the biosensor.
instructions: Cut paper into desired shape for the sensor.
Sputter-coat a gold/palladium layer using shadow masking.

protocol.title: Functionalization with Anti-CRP Antibodies
protocol.purpose: To functionalize the biosensor with antibodies specific to CRP.
instructions: Incubate the sensor with anti-CRP antibodies.
Rinse with distilled water and incubate with 2 mM mercaptohexanol.
acceptance_criteria: Successful binding of antibodies to the sensor surface.
safety_notes: Handle antibodies with care to avoid contamination.

protocol.title: Testing Sensitivity and Detection Time
protocol.purpose: To evaluate the biosensor's performance in detecting CRP.
instructions: Apply whole blood samples to the biosensor.
Measure electrochemical response and record detection time.
acceptance_criteria: Detection of CRP at concentrations below 0.5 mg/L within 10 minutes.
troubleshooting: Check sensor calibration and sample application.
safety_notes: Dispose of blood samples according to biohazard protocols.

executive_summary.objective: To determine if vehicle-matched DMSO controls reduce variability in HEK293 cytotoxicity assays compared to standard DMSO protocols.
executive_summary.experimental_strategy: Conduct parallel cytotoxicity assays using HEK293 cells with vehicle-matched DMSO controls and standard DMSO protocols, measuring variability in cytotoxicity.
executive_summary.expected_result: Vehicle-matched DMSO controls will reduce variability in cytotoxicity assays.
executive_summary.decision_gate: Determine if variability is significantly reduced with vehicle-matched controls.

waste_disposal: Dispose of DMSO waste according to chemical waste guidelines
critical_warnings: Ensure proper ventilation when handling DMSO

protocol.title: Cell Culture Preparation
protocol.purpose: To prepare HEK293 cells for cytotoxicity assays.
instructions: Thaw HEK293 cells and culture in DMEM with 10% heat-inactivated FBS (Sigma-Aldrich, catalog F4135).
Maintain cells at 37°C in a 5% CO2 incubator until 80% confluence.
troubleshooting: If contamination occurs, discard and restart with new cells.
safety_notes: Handle all cell culture materials in a biosafety cabinet.

protocol.title: DMSO Control Preparation
protocol.purpose: To prepare vehicle-matched and standard DMSO controls.
instructions: Prepare DMSO solutions matching the vehicle used in the experimental arm.
Ensure DMSO concentration does not exceed 0.5% v/v.
acceptance_criteria: DMSO concentration is consistent across all samples.
troubleshooting: Verify DMSO concentration with a spectrophotometer if discrepancies are suspected.

protocol.title: Cytotoxicity Assay Execution
protocol.purpose: To measure cytotoxicity in HEK293 cells with different DMSO controls.
instructions: Treat HEK293 cells with test compounds and corresponding DMSO controls.
Incubate for 24 hours at 37°C.
Measure cell viability using a suitable assay (e.g., MTT or Alamar Blue).
acceptance_criteria: Viability measurements are consistent with expected control values.
troubleshooting: Ensure all reagents are fresh and properly prepared.
safety_notes: Dispose of waste according to chemical safety guidelines.

evidence_cards.snippet: To observe GSH changes on adherent HEK-293 cells dosed with l-Buthionine Sulfoximine (BSO), we used the GSH/GSSG-Glo™ Assay to monitor total GSH levels (Figure 5). BSO is a known inhibitor of the synthesis of cellular GSH. Cells also were assayed for cytotoxicity effects of BSO using the CellTox™ Green Cytotoxicity Assay. Cells dosed with a serial dilution of BSO showed no cytotoxicity compared to the vehicle control, indicating that the compound did not kill the cells (Figure 5, Panel A). title: vehicle-matched DMSO controls
hypothesis: Test whether vehicle-matched DMSO controls reduce variability in HEK293 cytotoxicity assays.

novelty.rationale: The provided references do not contain any studies directly related to the use of vehicle-matched DMSO controls in HEK293 cytotoxicity assays. The references cover a range of topics including viral immunotherapy, protein degradation pathways, and various cellular mechanisms, but none address the specific hypothesis regarding DMSO controls and assay variability.

executive_summary.objective: To determine if vehicle-matched DMSO controls reduce variability in HEK293 cytotoxicity assays compared to standard DMSO protocols.
executive_summary.experimental_strategy: Conduct parallel cytotoxicity assays using HEK293 cells with vehicle-matched DMSO controls and standard DMSO protocols, measuring variability in cytotoxicity.
executive_summary.expected_result: Vehicle-matched DMSO controls will reduce variability in cytotoxicity assays.
executive_summary.decision_gate: Determine if variability is significantly reduced with vehicle-matched controls.

waste_disposal: Dispose of DMSO waste according to chemical waste guidelines
critical_warnings: Ensure proper ventilation when handling DMSO

protocol.title: Cell Culture Preparation
protocol.purpose: To prepare HEK293 cells for cytotoxicity assays.
instructions: Thaw HEK293 cells and culture in DMEM with 10% heat-inactivated FBS (Sigma-Aldrich, catalog F4135).
Maintain cells at 37°C in a 5% CO2 incubator until 80% confluence.
troubleshooting: If contamination occurs, discard and restart with new cells.
safety_notes: Handle all cell culture materials in a biosafety cabinet.

protocol.title: DMSO Control Preparation
protocol.purpose: To prepare vehicle-matched and standard DMSO controls.
instructions: Prepare DMSO solutions matching the vehicle used in the experimental arm.
Ensure DMSO concentration does not exceed 0.5% v/v.
acceptance_criteria: DMSO concentration is consistent across all samples.
troubleshooting: Verify DMSO concentration with a spectrophotometer if discrepancies are suspected.

protocol.title: Cytotoxicity Assay Execution
protocol.purpose: To measure cytotoxicity in HEK293 cells with different DMSO controls.
instructions: Treat HEK293 cells with test compounds and corresponding DMSO controls.
Incubate for 24 hours at 37°C.
Measure cell viability using a suitable assay (e.g., MTT or Alamar Blue).
acceptance_criteria: Viability measurements are consistent with expected control values.
troubleshooting: Ensure all reagents are fresh and properly prepared.
safety_notes: Dispose of waste according to chemical safety guidelines.

materials.name: HEK293 Cells
materials.notes: Ensure cells are authenticated and mycoplasma-free. Matched live supplier evidence: [PDF] HEK293 LgBiT Cell Line and LgBiT Expression Vector. equipment.purpose: To provide a sterile environment for cell culture.
equipment.notes: Use for all cell culture manipulations.

budget.calculation_notes: 3/3 materials use AI-estimated approximations (verify before ordering).  3/3 material costs known. Subtotal sums known costs only. 1 equipment item(s) assumed not available; cost added to budget. Contingency: 15%.

timeline.name: Preparation Phase
timeline.decision_gate: Verify cell confluence and DMSO preparation

controls.name: Vehicle-matched DMSO control
controls.purpose: To match the vehicle used in the experimental arm.

controls.name: Standard DMSO control
controls.purpose: To provide a baseline for comparison.

validation.sample_size_rationale: Triplicates ensure statistical power to detect variability differences.
validation.randomization_blinding: Randomize plate layout; blinding not applicable.
validation.statistical_analysis: ANOVA to compare variability between controls.
success_criteria: Significant reduction in variability with vehicle-matched controls.
data_quality_checks: Check for outliers and consistency across replicates.

risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Use aseptic techniques and regular mycoplasma testing.
risks_and_mitigations.detection_signal: Visual inspection and growth rate anomalies

assumptions.assumption: HEK293 cells will grow to confluence in one week.
DMSO concentration can be accurately prepared.

known_gaps: Lack of direct literature on vehicle-matched DMSO controls in HEK293 assays.

evidence_cards.snippet: Figure 4. Multiplexed assays using CellTiter 96® AQueous One Solution Cell Proliferation Assay and CellTox™ Green Cytotoxicity Assay. HEK 293 cells were treated for 14 hours with a serial dilution of terfenadine, followed by either a 1-hour (Panel A) or 4-hour (Panel B) treatment with CellTiter 96® AQueous One Solution Reagent and tested for viability and cytotoxicity. CellTox™ Green Dye was incorporated into the medium (no-step method) for half of the samples (+ CellTox™ Green Cytotoxicity Assay). Right axis represents fluorescent signal from CellTox™ Green Dye in relative fluorescent units …

evidence_cards.title: Differentiating Changes in Glutathione Levels from Cytotoxic Events Using Multiplexed Assays
evidence_cards.snippet: To observe GSH changes on adherent HEK-293 cells dosed with l-Buthionine Sulfoximine (BSO), we used the GSH/GSSG-Glo™ Assay to monitor total GSH levels (Figure 5). BSO is a known inhibitor of the synthesis of cellular GSH. Cells also were assayed for cytotoxicity effects of BSO using the CellTox™ Green Cytotoxicity Assay. Cells dosed with a serial dilution of BSO showed no cytotoxicity compared to the vehicle control, indicating that the compound did not kill the cells (Figure 5, Panel A). Adding the positive control, digitonin, resulted in a large increase in cytotoxic signal. [...] | | | | …

evidence_cards.title: Making Sense of Your Genetic Reporter Data Through Assay Multiplexing
evidence_cards.snippet: The graphs reveal the inverse concordance that is expected from the multiplex of the CellTox™ Green Cytotoxicity Assay (fluorescence, RFU) and the luciferase reporter assay (luminescence, RLU). Luminescent signal declines as cells die and produce less protein. Fluorescent signal rises as the cell membrane becomes compromised, allowing the CellTox™ Green Dye to bind to the DNA. Panels A, B, and C are the Bright-Glo™, Steady-Glo®, and ONE-Glo™ Assays performed on HEK293 cells producing firefly luciferase constitutively; panel D is the Renilla-Glo™ assay performed on HEK293 cells producing Renil…
